LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BOTH CLAIMS ALLEGED AGAINST THE STATE. IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGING THAT THEY SUBMITTED A TIMELY GOVERNMENT CLAIM TO THE STATE AND, IF THEY ARE ABLE TO DO SO, THEY ARE ALSO GIVEN LEAVE TO ALLEGE ULTIMATE FACTS SHOWING THAT THE NEGLIGENCE OF ONE OR MORE EMPLOYEES OF THE STATE WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING HARM TO THEM. PLAINTIFFS ARE REQUIRED TO BUT DID NOT ALLEGE THAT THEY SUBMITTED A TIMELY GOVERNMENT CLAIM TO THE STATE. PLAINTIFFS' NEGLIGENCE CLAIM FAILS BECAUSE IT APPEARS TO BE BASED ON DIRECT NEGLIGENCE OF THE STATE RATHER THAN VICARIOUS LIABILITY FOR THE NEGLIGENCE OF A STATE EMPLOYEE ACTING IN THE COURSE OF HIS OR HER EMPLOYMENT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-25-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Defendant State of California's demurrer to the second and third causes of action alleged in the complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to both claims alleged against the State. If they can do so in good faith, plaintiffs are given leave to file a first amended complaint alleging that they submitted a timely government claim to the State and, if they are able to do so, they are also given leave to allege ultimate facts showing that the negligence of one or more employees of the State was a substantial factor in causing harm to them. Plaintiffs are required to but did not allege that they submitted a timely government claim to the State. Plaintiffs' negligence claim fails because it appears to be based on direct negligence of the State rather than vicarious liability for the negligence of a State employee acting in the course of his or her employment. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
